description: Part of a letter written by Shelomo ‘the lesser, Head of the Yeshiva’ (i.e. the Gaʾon Shelomo b. Yehuda), in Jerusalem, to Efrayim b. Shemarya, in Fusṭāṭ. Dated: 1035 CE (per Gil) and, at the latest, 1035–1051 CE, based on the years when Shelomo b. Yehuda was in office after the events explained in the letter. Regarding the special collection to help restore ‘the cave’ (i.e. the Rabbanite synagogue in Jerusalem, that was damaged in the collapse of part of the Western Wall on the Temple Mount during Passover 1034 CE). The restoration of the synagogue is described, including the removal of rubble, and the use of 62 beams and 600 planks. Also mentions details concerning captives, as well as Yosef b. Yaʿqūb, a prominent member of the Fusṭāṭ Jewish community, who is loyal to the Babylonian academy of Pumbeditha and has the title Alūf. Greetings are sent to the addressee's son-in-law, Yosef. (Information from CUDL.)
tags: Efrayim b. Shemarya, Shelomo b. Yehuda
description_he: חלק ממכתב משלמה בן יהודה, ירושלים, לאפרים בן שמריה, 1035. על איסוף כספים לשיקום 'המערה' (בית הכנסת הרבני בירושלים) לאחר קריסת חלק מהכותל.
